title: If CONTAINER_HOST env variable is set default podman --remote=true
body:
Users enabling CONTAINER_HOST==PATH is indicating to podman they intend
to use remote functionality.

Fixes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11196

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Should we do the same for `CONTAINER_CONNECTION`?
Yes and that does not seem to be documented anywhere.

I do not think this works. Remote must be parsed before any cli commands are defined. The code handles this in a special way here: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/171f7b8975194ff9f47a3dd23adec81d11e6d104/cmd/podman/registry/remote.go#L20

This is because the cli changes depending if we are remote or not, so when cobra parsed the --remote flag it is already to late.
